title: This morning's headlines
overview: An automated roundup of the most recent stories across the Brief's sources. Add an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret and tomorrow's çay comes with a written briefing.

sections.heading: Türkiye
sections.body: Turkey’s overseas credit card spending hit record in May (Turkish Minute) · Turkey's Ozel launches new party to challenge Erdogan (DW.com) · Ousted Turkey opposition leader plans to create a new party (DW.com)

sections.heading: Around the region
sections.body: US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme (BBC) · Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist (BBC)

sections.heading: Migration & residency
sections.body: Germany has deported a Uyghur man to China, his mother says (myMotherLode.com)

sections.heading: Safety
sections.body: Trump says US will destroy a bridge or power plant for each Iranian attack in the Strait of Hormuz (AP News)

sections.heading: Money
sections.body: Turkey Rates to Stay Put as Middle East War Impedes Disinflation (Bloomberg.com) · Rising Inflation Expectations Threaten Turkish Lira Outlook (TradingPedia)

closing: Full stories in the feed below.
